title: 10 best herbal capsules for swollen feet

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel capsules helps with swollen feet because of its natural astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and ease swelling. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of witch hazel leaves, which have been used for centuries to treat various skin issues, including edema and water retention. As the capsules are absorbed into the bloodstream, they work to constrict blood vessels and reduce excess fluid accumulation in the feet, leaving them feeling softer, less puffy, and more comfortable. This natural remedy provides relief from the discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Witch Hazel contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet, making it easier to manage conditions like edema and arthritis.

Astringent and Constricting Properties: The tannins present in Witch Hazel have astringent properties, which can help reduce excess moisture and fluid buildup in the feet, reducing swelling and discomfort.

Antiseptic and Antibacterial Properties: The antimicrobial compounds in Witch Hazel can help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate foot conditions like athlete's foot, fungal nails, or sweaty feet, while also promoting healthy wound healing.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Hamamelis virginiana help reduce inflammation and swelling by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid buildup in the feet.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids in Hamamelis virginiana have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin, a type of tannin, is known for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and promote the healing of damaged skin on the feet.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used to make capsules due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and soothe the skin.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules because they contain tannins and other compounds that help reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and improve blood circulation in swollen feet.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (or rhizomes) are used to make capsules due to their rich content of salicylic acid, which acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and pain reliever, making them effective in treating swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs from a trusted supplier.
Crush the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 50g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the gelatin-glycerin mixture using a capsule filler for 30 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain salicin, a natural compound that reduces inflammation and relieves pain. When taken orally, it is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the affected area, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of white willow also help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing long-lasting relief from swollen feet. This makes white willow capsules a natural and effective solution for those suffering from chronic foot pain and swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White willow's salicin content helps to reduce inflammation, which can alleviate swelling and pain associated with swollen feet.
Analgesic: The salicin in white willow capsules also acts as a natural pain reliever, providing temporary relief from discomfort caused by swollen feet.
Antioxidant: White willow's antioxidant properties may help to protect the skin and underlying tissues of the feet from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2, and by acting as a natural pain reliever.

Salicylic acid: Reduces inflammation and swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, and by improving blood circulation, which can help reduce fluid buildup.
Quercetin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their diuretic properties, which can help reduce water retention and alleviate swelling.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to act as a natural anti-inflammatory, which can help reduce pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Dry 50 grams of salix alba bark in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.
Grind 20 grams of the dried salix alba bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 20 grams of a natural fiber such as gelatin to facilitate capsule formation.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 vegetable capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder-fiber mixture.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called aloin, which effectively reduces swelling and inflammation. The capsules also improve circulation by dilating blood vessels, allowing for better oxygen and nutrient delivery to the affected areas. Additionally, aloe vera's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and promote overall foot health, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating swollen feet and ankles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe capsules reduce inflammation in the body, which helps alleviate swelling in the feet by reducing redness and pain.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in herbal aloe capsules neutralize free radicals, which can cause oxidative stress and contribute to swelling and discomfort in the feet.

Wound-healing: Herbal aloe capsules promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue hydration, and enhancing blood flow to the affected area, all of which helps reduce swelling and accelerate recovery from foot injuries or conditions.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: These soluble fibers help reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet by preventing the absorption of excess fluids and promoting the removal of waste products.

Aloin and Anthraquinones: These anthraquinone glycosides have anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which help reduce swelling in the feet by promoting urine production and reducing fluid retention.

Vitamins and Minerals (such as Vitamin C, Vitamin E, and Potassium): These antioxidants and electrolytes help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote the removal of excess fluids, thereby alleviating swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most widely used part of Aloe vera due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce swelling.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from the leaves is used to make capsules as it contains vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help soothe and calm swollen feet.

Aloe-emodin (from Leaves): Aloe-emodin, a compound extracted from the leaves, is used in capsules due to its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which help alleviate swelling and discomfort in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of fresh aloe vera gel from the leaves of mature plants in the morning.
Wash the aloe vera gel with cool water and gently scrub off any impurities with a soft brush.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 6 hours.
Weigh and mix the dried aloe vera gel with 100 grams of vegetable capsule powder in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the mixture and produce 400 herbal aloe vera capsules in 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The capsules' active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and saponins, work together to improve circulation, relieve pressure on the feet, and diminish fluid retention. By targeting the root causes of swollen feet, these capsules provide long-lasting relief from pain, stiffness, and fatigue, allowing individuals to enjoy their daily activities without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas coneflower capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin, kaempferol, and luteolin, which help to reduce swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Kansas coneflower capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage foot tissues and contribute to swelling, promoting overall tissue health and reducing inflammation.

Vasodilatory effects: Kansas coneflower capsules contain compounds like ursolic acid, which helps to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing fluid buildup in the feet and ankles and alleviating swelling.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Alkaloids (Cichoric Acid and Cichorine): These alkaloids have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate swelling and pain in the feet by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Phenolic Acids (Chlorogenic Acid and Caffeic Acid): These compounds have vasodilatory properties that can help improve blood circulation and reduce fluid retention in the feet, thereby alleviating swelling and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of alkaloids, such as echinacein, which is believed to reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain echinacoside, a compound thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature echinacea angustifolia roots with a diameter of at least 1 inch and cut them into smaller sections.
Dry the echinacea angustifolia root sections in a single layer at 40 degrees fahrenheit for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried echinacea angustifolia root sections into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 1 part echinacea angustifolia powder with 4 parts cellulose powder in a bowl to create a filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the echinacea angustifolia mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with swollen feet because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fluid retention. The herb's diuretic effect stimulates urine production, thereby eliminating excess fluids and toxins that can contribute to swelling. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties help soothe and calm inflamed tissues, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing pressure on the feet. By taking herbal dandelion capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from swollen and puffy feet, improving their overall comfort and mobility.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion root and leaves contain compounds that increase urine production, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate swelling in the feet.
Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Dandelion contains flavonoids and taraxasterol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with swollen feet.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion capsules help protect the skin and tissues from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic swelling and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid saponin that helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Chicoric acid: A caffeic acid derivative that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate swelling and pain in the feet by reducing oxidative stress.
Inulin: A fructan polysaccharide that has diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce fluid retention in the feet and alleviate swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
Roots: Used as a diuretic to help reduce water retention and alleviate swelling in the feet.
Seeds: Used for their anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties to help reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale material in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 gram of the taraxacum officinale powder with 2 grams of gelatin to create a filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that cause inflammation. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can ease swelling and discomfort in the feet.
Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, particularly ursolic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate swelling and promote healing in the affected area.

Saponins: Saponins in Calendula officinalis have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help reduce swelling, prevent infection, and promote tissue repair, leading to faster recovery from swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling in the feet.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh calendula officinalis flowers at peak bloom and dry them completely in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 10000 rpm for 30 seconds.
Mix 5 grams of the calendula powder with 10 grams of cellulose powder in a stainless steel bowl.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture in 5 minutes.
Label and store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, this herbal extract can help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the body, including in the feet. This natural remedy can provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet, making it an effective complement to other treatments for conditions such as poor circulation, edema, and fluid retention. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants like hyperforin and hypericin, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, potentially relieving swelling in the feet.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herb is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, such as prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Vasodilatory effects: St. John's Wort contains chemicals like hypericin and pseudohypericin, which can cause blood vessels to dilate, potentially improving circulation and reducing swelling in the feet by allowing excess fluids to be reabsorbed more efficiently.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It helps alleviate swollen feet by reducing inflammation due to its potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Quercetin: It helps alleviate swollen feet by reducing fluid retention and alleviating the pain associated with inflammation and swelling.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain.
Stems: The stems are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in healing and soothing swollen feet.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a reputable source in the morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered plant material with 100 grams of gelatin powder and 50 grams of purified water to create a uniform mixture.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filling machine.

Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide): Bilobalide, a type of terpenoid, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the feet, while also improving microcirculation to promote healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer when they are fully ripe and have turned golden brown.

Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense capsules helps with swollen feet because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, frankincense extract reduces inflammation in the body, including swelling in the feet caused by excessive water retention, poor circulation, or underlying medical conditions. The capsules also stimulate the lymphatic system to drain excess fluids and reduce swelling, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet. Additionally, frankincense's natural astringent properties help to tighten skin tissue, reducing the appearance of swelling and promoting healthy foot function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, making it effective in alleviating painful and swollen feet.

remedy_constituents: Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA): AKBA has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
α- and β-boswellic acids (BAs): BAs exhibit anti-inflammatory and anti-arthritic effects, which help alleviate swollen feet by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
α-pinene: α-pinene, a terpene, has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help relieve pain and reduce swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are used due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of Boswellia serrata are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and pain in swollen feet.
Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are also used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in alleviating pain and discomfort in swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 500 grams of dried boswellia serrata root powder, with a 1:5 root to herb ratio.
Mix the boswellia serrata root powder with 1 liter of water and 1 tablespoon of gelatin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Fill gelatin capsules with the liquid extract, using a 1 ml fill volume, and seal immediately.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried eupatorium perfoliatum root and 1 part dried eupatorium perfoliatum leaf material in a container.
Mix the gathered materials together in a ratio of 1:1 for a consistent herbal blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal blend per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.
Label the container with the name eupatorium perfoliatum root and leaf capsules and the date prepared.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems of sufficient quantity for drying.
Dry the harvested material in a warm air circulation oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a mill or coffee grinder.
Mix one part powdered urtica dioica with two parts calcium carbonate and fill capsules with this blend.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried quercus robur bark according to good manufacturing practices within the next six months of use.
Grind 500g of dried quercus robur bark into a fine powder using a food processor for five minutes.
Mix 200g of powdered quercus robur with 100g of finely milled gelatin and 50g of magnesium stearate in a bowl for ten minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately using an encapsulation machine within two hours.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to one year after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and improve circulation. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of achillea millefolium and sift through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any debris or stems weighing 100 grams.
Combine the sifted leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container and let steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Measure out 250 milliliters of the filtered liquid and mix with 50 grams of gelatin powder in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the cooled gelatin mixture weighing approximately 500 milligrams each and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves on a sunny morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested herbs using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 3-4 hours or air-drying for 7-10 days.
Grind 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered sambucus nigra with 2 parts filler material such as potato starch to fill capsules.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in early morning dew and air-dry them thoroughly within two days.
Crush dried chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, taking about 15 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered chamomile with 500 milliliters of pure glycerin in a glass jar, stirring for five minutes.
Let the mixture steep in a cool dark place for eight hours to allow for gentle extraction and infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, squeezing out any remaining liquid and filling capsules with 250-500 milligram dried chamomile powder.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain the natural anti-inflammatory properties of lemon verbena, a calming herb that soothes irritated skin. As we age, our circulation can become sluggish, leading to fluid retention and swelling in the feet. The antioxidants and flavonoids in lemon verbena work to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, helping to alleviate this discomfort and leave feet feeling relaxed and refreshed. Regular use of lemon verbena capsules can also help to improve overall circulation, reducing the likelihood of future episodes of swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena capsules contain compounds that reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate swelling and discomfort in the feet caused by conditions like arthritis or poor circulation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in lemon verbena capsules help protect against free radical damage to tissues, which can contribute to swollen feet by reducing blood flow and damaging connective tissue.
Vasodilatory properties: Lemon verbena's vasodilatory effects can help improve circulation and reduce swelling in the feet by widening blood vessels and promoting healthy blood flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, barks have a soothing and calming effect on irritated skin, providing relief from swelling and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase dried ones at a reputable herb store.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried verbena leaves into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a mixture of 1 gram of powdered verbena and 2 grams of coconut oil, then seal the capsules.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with swollen feet because they provide a soothing and anti-inflammatory relief to the affected area. The herb, known for its mucilaginous properties, forms a protective barrier on the skin that reduces swelling and discomfort. It also contains antioxidants that help to break down excess fluid retention, further reducing puffiness. Additionally, marshmallow capsules can help to improve blood circulation, which aids in removing toxins and excess fluids that can contribute to swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal marshmallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like mucilage, which helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet by soothing and calming irritated tissues.

Emollient properties: Marshmallow root has emollient properties, which means it helps to soften and soothe dry, cracked skin on the feet, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with swollen feet.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds present in herbal marshmallow capsules help to protect the feet from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate swelling and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and reduce swelling in the feet by creating a protective barrier against irritation.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours of opening.
Measure out one part marshmallow root powder to two parts carrier agent such as rice flour or silica dioxide in a clean glass bowl.
Mix the powdered mixture thoroughly for at least five minutes until it forms a uniform blend with no lumps or clumps using a wooden spoon or silicone spatula.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 400-500 milligrams of the blended powder using a powder filler or small funnel within two hours of preparing the mixture.

Store the filled capsules in an airtight container made of dark glass or plastic at room temperature for up to six months and use as directed taking one to three capsules orally twice daily with water for relief from swollen feet.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This active ingredient reduces swelling by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and increasing blood flow to the affected area. Additionally, licorice root extract has been shown to help reduce water retention, which can contribute to swelling in the feet. As a result, herbal licorice capsules provide relief from uncomfortable swelling and pain, leaving your feet feeling refreshed and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory hormones.
Antioxidant properties: Herbal licorice capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage tissues and contribute to swelling and pain in the feet.
Diuretic properties: Licorice root has natural diuretic properties, which help increase urine production and reduce fluid retention in the body, including in the feet, thus alleviating swollen feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in swollen feet.
Rhyzomes: Licorice rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm swollen feet, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier weighing 250 grams.
Grind 200 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered roots with 50 grams of finely ground capsules shells and 100 grams of silica filler in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture weighing approximately 400 milligrams per capsule using a capsule filling machine for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature protected from light for 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain bioactive compounds that possess potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain by inhibiting prostaglandins, which are responsible for causing inflammation. Additionally, ginger's natural warming effect can help to increase blood circulation, which can further aid in reducing fluid retention and alleviating discomfort associated with swollen feet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the feet by blocking the production of prostaglandins, a chemical that causes pain and inflammation.
Pain relief properties: Ginger has natural analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help to numb the pain caused by swollen feet, making it easier to bear weight or walk comfortably.
Vasodilatory effects: Herbal ginger capsules can help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing fluid accumulation in the feet, which can contribute to swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with swollen feet.
Rhyzomes: Contains active ingredients like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective against swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger roots weighing approximately 500 grams for processing.
Wash the fresh ginger roots thoroughly under cold running water then gently scrub with a vegetable brush.
Cut the cleaned ginger roots into small pieces and dry them in a hot air dryer at 60 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Grind the dried ginger powder into a fine consistency using an electric spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the ground ginger powder then seal immediately for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for swollen feet are a natural remedy designed to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs that help to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and promote overall foot health. The best herbal capsules for swollen feet include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Ginkgo biloba, Equisetum arvense, Aesculus hippocastanum, Hamamelis virginiana, Curcuma longa, Ruscus aculeatus, Cinchona officinalis, Angelica sinensis, and Vitis vinifera. If left untreated, swollen feet can lead to chronic pain, limited mobility, and a decreased quality of life, making it difficult to perform daily activities and enjoy hobbies.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen feet that are treated with herbal capsules are varicose veins, edema, and poor circulation. Herbal formulas containing ingredients like Ginkgo biloba, Horse Chestnut, and Butcher's Broom help alleviate swelling by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation. Other herbs such as Turmeric and Ginger also provide anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm the affected areas.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with swollen feet is a blend of turmeric, ginger, and arnica. Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation and pain, while ginger's warming effect promotes blood circulation and relaxation. Arnica's natural anti-inflammatory compounds further reduce swelling and promote healing. Taking these capsules together can provide relief from swollen feet, reducing discomfort and promoting overall well-being. Regular use may also prevent future episodes of swelling and promote healthy foot function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with swollen feet because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain. The licorice root extract in these capsules has been traditionally used to alleviate water retention and promote fluid balance in the body. Additionally, it has been shown to improve circulation and reduce edema, making it an effective natural remedy for swollen feet. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules can provide long-lasting relief from swollen and tired feet.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid Saponins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines that contribute to fluid retention in the feet.

Glycyrrhizic Acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate swelling and pain in the feet by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the production of prostaglandins, which contribute to inflammation.

Flavonoids (specifically, Licoflavone C): These compounds have vasodilatory effects that help to improve blood circulation and reduce edema in the feet by relaxing blood vessels and increasing the removal of excess fluid from the affected area.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which helps to reduce inflammation and swelling.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to relax the muscles and reduce swelling.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate swelling and pain in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable supplier to ensure quality.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Mix the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 10 milliliters of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years to maintain potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with swollen feet because they improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to break down excess fluid in the feet and ankles. By enhancing blood flow and reducing swelling, Ginkgo biloba capsules can provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with swollen feet, making it an effective natural remedy for this common issue. Regular use can lead to noticeable improvements in foot health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate swollen feet by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which can help in reducing the fluid buildup in the feet.
Bilobalide: Contribute to swollen feet relief by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune system, and improving the structural integrity of the blood vessels, thereby reducing swelling.
Ginkgolides: Aid in swollen feet relief by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory mediators, which can contribute to the reduction of inflammation and swelling in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to help improve circulation and reduce swelling in the feet.
Seeds: The seeds contain bilobalide, a compound thought to aid in reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected areas.
Buds: The buds contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to improve circulation, reduce swelling, and promote healing in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 200g of the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed blender.
Mix 1g of the ginkgo biloba powder with 1g of gelatin and 2g of water in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill 100 gelatin capsules each with 1g of the ginkgo biloba mixture.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain Horsetail, a plant rich in silica, which helps to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The silica in Horsetail also acts as a natural diuretic, helping to flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body. This can lead to a reduction in swelling and discomfort in the feet. Regular use of Equisetum arvense capsules can also help to improve the overall health of the skin and connective tissues, reducing the appearance of swollen feet.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the feet by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Flavonoids: They act as potent antioxidants, which help to reduce oxidative stress and improve blood circulation, thereby alleviating swelling and pain in the feet.
Polysaccharides: They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the feet by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their ability to reduce swelling and ease pain.
Stems: The stems are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-itching properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried equisetum arvense roots and stems from a trusted source.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powdered equisetum arvense with 2 tablespoons of gelatin in a bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the equisetum arvense and gelatin mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal equisetum arvense capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain horse chestnut extract, which is rich in escin and other flavonoids. These compounds help to reduce fluid retention and inflammation in the feet, alleviating swelling and discomfort. By improving blood flow and reducing permeability of blood vessels, Aesculus hippocastanum capsules can also help to prevent further swelling and promote a reduction in pain and stiffness associated with swollen feet. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective solution for managing swollen feet.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help to reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the feet by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Flavonoids: Act as powerful antioxidants, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area, thus providing relief from swelling and discomfort.
Phytosterols: Help to improve blood circulation and reduce fluid accumulation in the feet, thereby alleviating swelling and promoting overall vascular health.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: They are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for swollen feet due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aesculus hippocastanum seeds and roots from mature plants at the end of the growing season after 2 years.
Dry the harvested parts in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours using a dehydrator.
Grind 250 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum roots and 50 grams of seeds into a fine powder using a mortar.
Mix 1200 mg of the ground aesculus hippocastanum powder with 100 mg of cellulose powder and 100 mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill 50-capsule shells with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain the extract of the Witch Hazel plant, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, work to reduce swelling and promote healthy blood flow to the affected area. By reducing inflammation and improving circulation, Hamamelis virginiana capsules can help alleviate swollen feet, providing relief from discomfort, pain, and fatigue associated with this common condition. Regular use may also promote overall foot health.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help with swollen feet by reducing inflammation and constricting blood vessels, which in turn decreases fluid accumulation and swelling.
Flavonoids: They help with swollen feet by improving blood flow, reducing vascular permeability, and inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for inflammation and swelling.
Astringent phenolic acids: They help with swollen feet by constricting blood vessels and reducing fluid accumulation, which leads to decreased swelling and relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort.
Barks: They are used due to their astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana twigs and leaves from the plant's above-ground parts.
Dry the harvested plant material for 24 hours at 50 degrees celsius in a drying oven.
Grind 50 grams of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 1 gram of gelatin in a small bowl and add 10 milliliters of distilled water.
Encapsulate the mixture into empty vegetable capsules using a capsule filler and a dropper bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with swollen feet because of their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the feet. The active compound curcumin in these capsules has excellent bioavailability, allowing it to penetrate deep into the tissues and joints. This helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with swollen feet, such as redness, warmth, and stiffness. Regular use of these capsules can lead to improved circulation, reduced fluid retention, and overall relief from swollen feet.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps reduce swelling by inhibiting inflammatory pathways and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort associated with swollen feet.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet by neutralizing free radicals and preventing oxidative stress.

Tumerone (also known as beta-curcumen): Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative effects, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the feet by inhibiting inflammatory cytokines and promoting the breakdown of inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are used because they contain a mixture of bioactive compounds, including curcumin, that help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling.
Stems: Stems are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried curcuma longa roots and store them in a well-ventilated area for 2 days.
Grind 50g of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 100g of gelatin in a bowl and add 100ml of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 80°c for 30 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill 20 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the prepared mixture each and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce fluid retention and swelling. The capsules also aid in improving blood circulation, which helps to flush out excess fluids and reduce puffiness. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus capsules may help to reduce the appearance of varicose veins and improve overall circulation, making them an effective natural remedy for alleviating swollen feet. Regular use can provide long-lasting relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Steroidal saponins: They have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the feet.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting blood circulation, thus alleviating swollen feet.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and vascular protective effects, which may help in reducing fluid retention and improving blood circulation in the feet, thereby alleviating swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are often used in traditional medicine due to their perceived therapeutic properties.
Leaves: In some herbal remedies, leaves are used for their supposed anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects.
Roots: They are commonly used for various medicinal purposes, including potentially treating swollen feet.
remedy_recipe: Gather ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems in quantities of about 100g.
Dry the ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ruscus aculeatus leaves and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the ruscus aculeatus powder with 250mg of a natural filler in a small bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and alleviates pain. The capsules also have a diuretic effect, which helps to flush out excess fluids and promote circulation, reducing the appearance of swelling. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis capsules have a mild anticoagulant property that prevents blood clots from forming, which can contribute to swollen feet. Overall, Cinchona officinalis capsules provide a natural and effective solution for managing swollen feet.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Quinine helps reduce swelling in feet by its anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen feet.
Cinchonine: Cinchonine has diuretic properties that help reduce fluid retention in the body, thereby reducing swelling in the feet.
Quinidine: Quinidine exhibits anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which aid in reducing swelling and promoting blood circulation in the feet.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Cinchona officinalis due to their high quinine content, which helps to reduce swelling in the feet.
Barks: The barks are used to make capsules for swollen feet because they contain quinine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps to relieve swelling and pain.
Fruits: The fruits are also used, although less commonly, to make capsules for swollen feet due to their quinine content, which helps to reduce inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark, ensuring it is free from contamination and moisture, in the morning when the plant is dry.
Dry the harvested cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 250mg of the ground cinchona officinalis powder with 500mg of cellulose and 50mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain dong quai, a herbal remedy that promotes blood circulation and reduces inflammation. The capsules also contain antioxidants and other nutrients that help to alleviate swelling and discomfort in the feet. Additionally, dong quai is believed to have diuretic properties, which can help to reduce water retention and alleviate swelling. As a result, taking Angelica sinensis capsules may provide relief from swollen feet and improve overall foot health.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: It helps reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can alleviate swelling in the feet.
Butylphthalide: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and relieve pain associated with swollen feet.
Z- Ligustilide: It has vasodilatory effects, which can improve blood circulation and reduce swelling in the feet.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high concentration of ferulic acid and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with swollen feet.
Leaves: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate swelling and discomfort in the feet.
Stems: Employed for their similar anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to leaves, helping to reduce swelling and promote overall foot health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh or dried angelica sinensis roots with at least 5% volatile oil content.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2-3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at 3000 rpm for 2 minutes.
Mix 1-2 teaspoons of the powdered angelica sinensis with 1 teaspoon of cellulose or other natural capsule filler.
Fill 100-200 count gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a manual capsule filler within 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera capsules helps with swollen feet because they contain extract from the grapevine, rich in resveratrol, an antioxidant with anti-inflammatory properties. This reduces swelling and pain in the feet by alleviating fluid retention and improving blood circulation. Regular use of Vitis vinifera capsules may also help to tone the veins and arteries, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing the risk of conditions like varicose veins, which can contribute to swollen feet.
remedy_constituents: Resveratrol: This polyphenol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with swollen feet.
Proanthocyanidins: These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate swelling in the feet.

Oligomeric Proanthocyanidin (OPC): As a powerful antioxidant, OPC can help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate swelling in the feet by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting healthy blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the feet.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm swollen feet.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their diuretic properties, which can help reduce water retention and alleviate swelling in the feet.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500g of dried vitis vinifera leaves using a solvent like ethanol or glycerin in a 1:5 ratio for 2 hours.
Filter the extracted mixture through a cheesecloth to remove the solids and obtain a clear liquid extract in 30 minutes.
Dry the filtered liquid extract using a rotary evaporator at 50°c to remove excess solvent for 2 hours.
Mix the dried extract with a binding agent like gelatin or cellulose in a 1:1 ratio to form a powder for 10 minutes.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with the powder and seal them using a capsule filler machine in 10 minutes.
